name: business-ethics-and-governance
description: Business ethics, corporate governance, and stakeholder responsibility for firms operating in complex social and regulatory environments. Covers ethical frameworks applied to business, stakeholder vs shareholder theory, board structure and fiduciary duty, conflicts of interest, whistleblowing, CSR and ESG, and the distinction between legal compliance and ethical conduct. Use when evaluating a decision with ethical stakes, designing a governance structure, or diagnosing a corporate scandal.
